pairs.question: Who was Albert Thoroughgood Buick and which football clubs did he play for during his career?
pairs.answer: Albert Thoroughgood Buick (17 January 1875 – 25 March 1948) was a Scottish footballer who played for Arbroath, Heart of Midlothian (Hearts), and Portsmouth during his career. He began at his hometown club Arbroath F.C. before moving to Hearts in 1896, and later joined Portsmouth in 1903.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Albert Buick accomplish with Heart of Midlothian?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick won the 1896-97 Scottish League title with Hearts and was part of the team that won the 1900-01 Scottish Cup, defeating Celtic 4-3 in the final. He also served as club captain during the 1902-03 season when Hearts reached the Scottish Cup final.

pairs.question: How did Albert Buick earn the nickname 'spider' and what physical attributes contributed to this?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick earned the nickname 'spider' due to his tall, gangly arms, slight stoop, and relatively short stature of 5 foot 7 inches. Despite his unconventional appearance for a defender, his stamina and all-action playing style made him a respected player.

pairs.question: What was significant about Albert Buick's international career with the Scotland national team?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick earned two caps for Scotland in March 1902, scoring in both appearances. Scotland won 5-1 against Ireland and 5-1 against Wales, though his selection was partly influenced by the stature of his contemporary rival, Alex Raisbeck.

pairs.question: How did Albert Buick contribute to Portsmouth's history during his time at the club?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick moved to Portsmouth in 1903 and spent eight seasons with the club. One of his most notable contributions was helping Portsmouth to an upset victory over Manchester United in a 1906-07 FA Cup replay.

pairs.question: What was the context of Albert Buick's transfer from Hearts to Portsmouth in 1903?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick transferred to Portsmouth in 1903, joining six former Hearts teammates in a move south to the Southern Football League side. This transfer marked a significant shift in his career as he moved from a Scottish club to an English-based team.

pairs.question: Why might Albert Buick not have won more honours despite his skills?
pairs.answer: Albert Buick may not have won more honours due to competition for the centre-half position from Alex Raisbeck, who was his contemporary and rival. Despite his two international goals and contributions to Hearts' success, Raisbeck's stature and performance limited Buick's opportunities for additional accolades.
